@router.put("/subscriptions")
async def upsert_subscription(
    context: Context,
    *,
    subscription: Subscription,
) -> Subscription:
    row = await context.database.fetchrow(
        """\
INSERT INTO subscriptions (
    id,
    account_id,
    billing_periods,
    created_at,
    price_id,
    status,
    stripe_subscription_id,
    stripe_subscription_item_id
)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3::jsonb, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
ON CONFLICT (stripe_subscription_item_id) DO UPDATE SET
    account_id = EXCLUDED.account_id,
    billing_periods = EXCLUDED.billing_periods,
    price_id = EXCLUDED.price_id,
    status = EXCLUDED.status,
    stripe_subscription_id = EXCLUDED.stripe_subscription_id
RETURNING
    id,
    account_id,
    billing_periods,
    created_at,
    price_id,
    status,
    stripe_subscription_id,
    stripe_subscription_item_id
""",
        subscription.id,
        subscription.account_id,
        json.dumps([period.model_dump(mode="json") for period in subscription.billing_periods]),
        subscription.created_at,
        subscription.price_id,
        subscription.status,
        subscription.stripe_subscription_id,
        subscription.stripe_subscription_item_id,
    )

    return Subscription(
        id=row["id"],
        account_id=row["account_id"],
        billing_periods=[Period(**period) for period in json.loads(row["billing_periods"])],
        created_at=row["created_at"],
        price_id=row["price_id"],
        status=row["status"],
        stripe_subscription_id=row["stripe_subscription_id"],
        stripe_subscription_item_id=row["stripe_subscription_item_id"],
    )


@router.get("/usages")
async def list_usages(
    context: Context,
    *,
    account_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
    as_of: datetime.datetime | None = None,
    email: str | None = None,
    metric: Metric | None = None,
) -> list[Usage]:
    as_of = as_of or datetime.datetime.now(datetime.UTC)

    domain = email.split("@")[-1] if email else None

    metrics = [metric.value] if metric else [m.value for m in Metric]

    rows = await context.database.fetch(
        """\
SELECT
    accounts.id AS account_id,
    accounts.created_at AS account_created_at,
    accounts.domains AS account_domains,
    accounts.members AS account_members,
    accounts.name AS account_name,
    accounts.owner AS account_owner,
    accounts.priority AS account_priority,
    accounts.stripe_customer_id AS account_stripe_customer_id,
    subscriptions.id AS subscription_id,
    subscriptions.billing_periods AS subscription_billing_periods,
    subscriptions.created_at AS subscription_created_at,
    subscriptions.price_id AS subscription_price_id,
    subscriptions.status AS subscription_status,
    subscriptions.stripe_subscription_id,
    subscriptions.stripe_subscription_item_id,
    prices.id AS price_id,
    prices.amount AS price_amount,
    prices.created_at AS price_created_at,
    prices.currency AS price_currency,
    prices.description AS price_description,
    prices.stripe_price_id,
    prices.stripe_product_id,
    metrics.metric,
    COALESCE(usage_counts.usage, 0) AS usage,
    quotas.id AS quota_id,
    quotas.created_at AS quota_created_at,
    quotas.hard_limit,
    quotas.is_active AS quota_is_active,
    quotas.soft_limit,
    billing_period.starts_at,
    billing_period.ends_at
FROM subscriptions
JOIN accounts ON accounts.id = subscriptions.account_id
JOIN prices ON prices.id = subscriptions.price_id
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT
        (period->>'starts_at')::timestamptz as starts_at,
        (period->>'ends_at')::timestamptz as ends_at
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(subscriptions.billing_periods) as period
    WHERE (period->>'starts_at')::timestamptz <= $2
      AND (period->>'ends_at')::timestamptz > $2
    LIMIT 1
) billing_period ON true
CROSS JOIN unnest($4::text[]) AS metrics(metric)
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as usage
    FROM usage_events
    WHERE usage_events.subscription_id = subscriptions.id
      AND usage_events.created_at >= billing_period.starts_at
      AND usage_events.created_at < billing_period.ends_at
      AND usage_events.metric = metrics.metric
) usage_counts ON billing_period.starts_at IS NOT NULL
LEFT JOIN quotas
       ON quotas.price_id = subscriptions.price_id
      AND quotas.metric = metrics.metric
      AND quotas.is_active = true
WHERE ($1::uuid IS NULL OR subscriptions.account_id = $1)
  AND billing_period.starts_at IS NOT NULL
  AND ($3::text IS NULL
       OR $3::text = accounts.owner
       OR $3::text = ANY(accounts.members)
       OR $5::text = ANY(accounts.domains))
""",
        account_id,
        as_of,
        email,
        metrics,
        domain,
    )

    return [
        Usage(
            account=Account(
                id=row["account_id"],
                created_at=row["account_created_at"],
                domains=row["account_domains"],
                members=row["account_members"],
                name=row["account_name"],
                owner=row["account_owner"],
                priority=row["account_priority"],
                stripe_customer_id=row["account_stripe_customer_id"],
            ),
            billing_period=Period(
                starts_at=row["starts_at"],
                ends_at=row["ends_at"],
            ),
            metric=Metric[row["metric"]],
            price=Price(
                id=row["price_id"],
                amount=row["price_amount"],
                created_at=row["price_created_at"],
                currency=row["price_currency"],
                description=row["price_description"],
                stripe_price_id=row["stripe_price_id"],
                stripe_product_id=row["stripe_product_id"],
            ),
            quota=(
                Quota(
                    id=row["quota_id"],
                    created_at=row["quota_created_at"],
                    hard_limit=row["hard_limit"],
                    is_active=row["quota_is_active"],
                    metric=Metric[row["metric"]],
                    price_id=row["price_id"],
                    soft_limit=row["soft_limit"],
                )
                if row["quota_id"] is not None
                else None
            ),
            subscription=Subscription(
                id=row["subscription_id"],
                account_id=row["account_id"],
                billing_periods=[
                    Period(**period) for period in json.loads(row["subscription_billing_periods"])
                ],
                created_at=row["subscription_created_at"],
                price_id=row["subscription_price_id"],
                status=row["subscription_status"],
                stripe_subscription_id=row["stripe_subscription_id"],
                stripe_subscription_item_id=row["stripe_subscription_item_id"],
            ),
            usage=row["usage"],
        )
        for row in rows
    ]


@router.get("/usage-events")
async def list_usage_events(
    context: Context,
    *,
    email: str | None = None,
    metric: Metric | None = None,
    subscription_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
) -> list[UsageEvent]:
    rows = await context.database.fetch(
        """\
SELECT id, created_at, email, metric, subscription_id
FROM usage_events
WHERE ($1::text IS NULL OR email = $1)
  AND ($2::text IS NULL OR metric = $2)
  AND ($3::uuid IS NULL OR subscription_id = $3)
ORDER BY created_at DESC
""",
        email,
        metric.value if metric else None,
        subscription_id,
    )

    return [
        UsageEvent(
            id=row["id"],
            created_at=row["created_at"],
            email=row["email"],
            metric=Metric[row["metric"]],
            subscription_id=row["subscription_id"],
        )
        for row in rows
    ]


@router.post("/usage-events")
async def upsert_usage_event(
    context: Context,
    *,
    usage_event: UsageEvent,
) -> UsageEvent:
    row = await context.database.fetchrow(
        """\
INSERT INTO usage_events (id, created_at, email, metric, subscription_id)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)
RETURNING id, created_at, email, metric, subscription_id
""",
        usage_event.id,
        usage_event.created_at,
        usage_event.email,
        usage_event.metric.name,
        usage_event.subscription_id,
    )

    return UsageEvent(
        id=row["id"],
        created_at=row["created_at"],
        email=row["email"],
        metric=row["metric"],
        subscription_id=row["subscription_id"],
    )


@router.get("/upgrades")
async def list_upgrades(
    context: Context,
    *,
    is_active: bool | None = None,
    stripe_product_id: str | None = None,
) -> list[Upgrade]:
    rows = await context.database.fetch(
        """\
SELECT
    upgrades.id,
    upgrades.created_at,
    upgrades.current_price_id,
    upgrades.is_active,
    upgrades.upgrade_price_id
FROM upgrades
JOIN prices ON prices.id = upgrades.upgrade_price_id
WHERE ($1::bool IS NULL OR upgrades.is_active = $1)
  AND ($2::text IS NULL OR prices.stripe_product_id = $2)
""",
        is_active,
        stripe_product_id,
    )

    return [
        Upgrade(
            id=row["id"],
            created_at=row["created_at"],
            current_price_id=row["current_price_id"],
            is_active=row["is_active"],
            upgrade_price_id=row["upgrade_price_id"],
        )
        for row in rows
    ]


@router.put("/upgrades")
async def upsert_upgrade(
    context: Context,
    *,
    upgrade: Upgrade,
) -> Upgrade:
    row = await context.database.fetchrow(
        """\
INSERT INTO upgrades (id, created_at, current_price_id, is_active, upgrade_price_id)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)
ON CONFLICT (current_price_id, upgrade_price_id) DO UPDATE SET
    is_active = EXCLUDED.is_active
RETURNING id, created_at, current_price_id, is_active, upgrade_price_id
""",
        upgrade.id,
        upgrade.created_at,
        upgrade.current_price_id,
        upgrade.is_active,
        upgrade.upgrade_price_id,
    )

    return Upgrade(
        id=row["id"],
        created_at=row["created_at"],
        current_price_id=row["current_price_id"],
        is_active=row["is_active"],
        upgrade_price_id=row["upgrade_price_id"],
    )

1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from "react";
2	import * as Form from "@radix-ui/react-form";
3	import {
4	  Button,
5	  Callout,

11	} from "@radix-ui/themes";
12	import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from "@tanstack/react-query";
13	import { v4 as uuidv4 } from "uuid";
14	
15	import { AlertCircleIcon } from "../../../../../infrastructure/ts/icons/AlertCircleIcon";
16	import { Loader2Icon } from "../../../../../infrastructure/ts/icons/Loader2Icon";
17	import { PlusIcon } from "../../../../../infrastructure/ts/icons/PlusIcon";
18	import { SaveIcon } from "../../../../../infrastructure/ts/icons/SaveIcon";

20	import { XIcon } from "../../../../../infrastructure/ts/icons/XIcon";
21	import type { Account } from "../../../client/ts";
22	import {
23	  listAccountsApiAccountsGetQueryKey,
24	  upsertAccountApiAccountsPutMutation,
25	} from "../../../client/ts/@tanstack/react-query.gen";
26	
27	type Props = {
28	  account: Account | null;
29	  isOpen: boolean;
30	  onClose: () => void;
31	};
32	
33	function getDefaultAccount(): Account {
34	  return {
35	    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
36	    id: uuidv4(),
37	    name: "",
38	    owner: "",
39	    priority: 0,
40	    domains: [],
41	    members: [],
42	  };
43	}
44	
45	export function AccountEditor({ account, isOpen, onClose }: Props) {
46	  const queryClient = useQueryClient();
47	
48	  const [draft, setDraft] = useState(getDefaultAccount());
49	
50	  const { mutate, reset, isPending, error } = useMutation({
51	    ...upsertAccountApiAccountsPutMutation(),
52	    onSuccess: () => {
53	      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
54	        queryKey: listAccountsApiAccountsGetQueryKey(),
55	      });
56	      onClose();
57	    },
58	  });
59	
60	  useEffect(() => {
61	    if (isOpen) {
62	      setDraft(account ?? getDefaultAccount());
63	      reset();
64	    }
65	  }, [account, isOpen, reset]);
66	
67	  const handleSubmit = useCallback(
68	    (e: React.FormEvent) => {
69	      e.preventDefault();
70	      mutate({
71	        body: {
72	          ...draft,
73	          domains: draft.domains?.filter(Boolean) ?? [],
74	          members: draft.members?.filter(Boolean) ?? [],
75	        },
76	      });
77	    },
78	    [draft, mutate],
79	  );
80	
81	  const canSubmit = Boolean(draft.name && draft.owner);
82	
83	  return (
84	    <Dialog.Root open={isOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => !open && onClose()}>
85	      <Dialog.Content maxWidth="480px">
86	        <Dialog.Title>
87	          {account ? "Edit Account" : "Create Account"}
88	        </Dialog.Title>
89	
90	        <Form.Root onSubmit={handleSubmit} onClearServerErrors={reset}>
91	          <Flex direction="column" gap="4" py="4">
92	            {error && (
93	              <Callout.Root color="red" size="1">
94	                <Callout.Icon>
95	                  <AlertCircleIcon size={16} />
96	                </Callout.Icon>
97	                <Callout.Text style={{ whiteSpace: "pre-wrap" }}>
98	                  {JSON.stringify(error, null, 2)}
99	                </Callout.Text>
100	              </Callout.Root>
101	            )}
102	
103	            <Form.Field name="created_at">
104	              <Form.Label asChild>
105	                <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
106	                  Created At
107	                </Text>
108	              </Form.Label>
109	              <Form.Control asChild>
110	                <TextField.Root value={draft.created_at} disabled />
111	              </Form.Control>
112	            </Form.Field>
113	
114	            <Form.Field name="id">
115	              <Form.Label asChild>
116	                <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
117	                  Account ID
118	                </Text>
119	              </Form.Label>
120	              <Form.Control asChild>
121	                <TextField.Root value={draft.id} disabled />
122	              </Form.Control>
123	            </Form.Field>
124	
125	            <Form.Field name="name">
126	              <Form.Label asChild>
127	                <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
128	                  Name <Text color="red">*</Text>
129	                </Text>
130	              </Form.Label>
131	              <Form.Control asChild>
132	                <TextField.Root
133	                  value={draft.name ?? ""}
134	                  onChange={(e) => setDraft({ ...draft, name: e.target.value })}
135	                  disabled={!!account}
136	                  placeholder="Example, Inc."
137	                />
138	              </Form.Control>
139	            </Form.Field>
140	
141	            {account?.stripe_customer_id && (
142	              <Form.Field name="stripe_customer_id">
143	                <Form.Label asChild>
144	                  <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
145	                    Stripe Customer ID
146	                  </Text>
147	                </Form.Label>
148	                <Form.Control asChild>
149	                  <TextField.Root value={account.stripe_customer_id} disabled />
150	                </Form.Control>
151	              </Form.Field>
152	            )}
153	
154	            <Form.Field name="owner">
155	              <Form.Label asChild>
156	                <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
157	                  Owner <Text color="red">*</Text>
158	                </Text>
159	              </Form.Label>
160	              <Form.Control asChild>
161	                <TextField.Root
162	                  value={draft.owner}
163	                  onChange={(e) =>
164	                    setDraft({ ...draft, owner: e.target.value })
165	                  }
166	                  placeholder="user@example.com"
167	                />
168	              </Form.Control>
169	            </Form.Field>
170	
171	            <Form.Field name="priority">
172	              <Form.Label asChild>
173	                <Text size="2" weight="medium" mb="1">
174	                  Priority
175	                </Text>
176	              </Form.Label>
177	              <Form.Control asChild>
178	                <TextField.Root
179	                  defaultValue={draft.priority?.toString() ?? "0"}
180	                  key={draft.id}
181	                  onBlur={(e) => {
182	                    const parsed = parseInt(e.target.value, 10);
183	                    setDraft({

185	                      priority: Number.isNaN(parsed) ? 0 : parsed,
186	                    });
187	                  }}
188	                  type="number"
189	                />
190	              </Form.Control>
191	            </Form.Field>
192	
193	            <Flex direction="column" gap="2">
194	              <Text size="2" weight="medium">
195	                Domains
196	              </Text>
197	              {(draft.domains ?? []).map((value, index) => (
198	                <Flex key={index} gap="2" align="center">
199	                  <TextField.Root
200	                    value={value}
201	                    onChange={(e) => {
202	                      const updated = [...(draft.domains ?? [])];

205	                    }}
206	                    placeholder="example.com"
207	                    style={{ flex: 1 }}
208	                  />
209	                  <IconButton
210	                    type="button"

212	                    color="gray"
213	                    onClick={() =>
214	                      setDraft({

217	                          (_, i) => i !== index,
218	                        ),
219	                      })
220	                    }
221	                  >
222	                    <XIcon size={16} />
223	                  </IconButton>
224	                </Flex>
225	              ))}
226	              <Button
227	                type="button"
228	                variant="ghost"
229	                size="1"
230	                style={{ alignSelf: "flex-start" }}
231	                onClick={() =>
232	                  setDraft({
233	                    ...draft,
234	                    domains: [...(draft.domains ?? []), ""],
235	                  })
236	                }
237	              >
238	                <PlusIcon size={14} /> Add domain
239	              </Button>
240	            </Flex>
241	
242	            <Flex direction="column" gap="2">
243	              <Text size="2" weight="medium">
244	                Members
245	              </Text>
246	              {(draft.members ?? []).map((value, index) => (
247	                <Flex key={index} gap="2" align="center">
248	                  <TextField.Root
249	                    value={value}
250	                    onChange={(e) => {
251	                      const updated = [...(draft.members ?? [])];
252	                      updated[index] = e.target.value;
253	                      setDraft({ ...draft, members: updated });
254	                    }}
255	                    placeholder="user@example.com"
256	                    style={{ flex: 1 }}
257	                  />
258	                  <IconButton
259	                    type="button"
260	                    variant="ghost"
261	                    color="gray"
262	                    onClick={() =>
263	                      setDraft({
264	                        ...draft,
265	                        members: (draft.members ?? []).filter(
266	                          (_, i) => i !== index,
267	                        ),
268	                      })
269	                    }
270	                  >
271	                    <XIcon size={16} />
272	                  </IconButton>
273	                </Flex>
274	              ))}
275	              <Button
276	                type="button"
277	                variant="ghost"
278	                size="1"
279	                style={{ alignSelf: "flex-start" }}
280	                onClick={() =>
281	                  setDraft({
282	                    ...draft,
283	                    members: [...(draft.members ?? []), ""],
284	                  })
285	                }
286	              >
287	                <PlusIcon size={14} /> Add member
288	              </Button>
289	            </Flex>
290	          </Flex>
291	
292	          <Flex gap="3" justify="end" mt="4">
293	            <Dialog.Close>
294	              <Button
295	                variant="soft"
296	                color="gray"
297	                type="button"
298	                style={{ width: 100 }}
299	              >
300	                <Trash2Icon size={14} />
301	                Cancel
302	              </Button>
303	            </Dialog.Close>
304	            <Form.Submit asChild>
305	              <Button
306	                type="submit"
307	                disabled={!canSubmit || isPending}
308	                style={{ width: 100 }}

310	                {isPending ? (
311	                  <Loader2Icon size={16} className="animate-spin" />
312	                ) : (
313	                  <>
314	                    <SaveIcon size={14} />
315	                    Save
316	                  </>
317	                )}
318	              </Button>
319	            </Form.Submit>
320	          </Flex>
321	        </Form.Root>
322	      </Dialog.Content>
323	    </Dialog.Root>
324	  );
325	}

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, overload

from scripts.call_claude import call_claude

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from scripts.load_config import Config


@overload
def call_mcp(
    config: Config,
    tool: str,
    input: dict[str, str],
    *,
    json_schema: dict[str, Any],
    timeout: int | None = ...,
) -> dict[str, Any]: ...


@overload
def call_mcp(
    config: Config,
    tool: str,
    input: dict[str, str],
    *,
    json_schema: None = ...,
    timeout: int | None = ...,
) -> str: ...


def call_mcp(
    config: Config,
    tool: str,
    input: dict[str, str],
    *,
    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    timeout: int | None = None,
) -> str | dict[str, Any]:
    args = "\n".join(
        [
            "- `input`:",
            *(f"    - `{k}`: {v}" for k, v in input.items()),
        ],
    )

    return call_claude(
        config=config,
        prompt=f"""\
Call the `mcp__plugin_softlight_softlight__{tool}` MCP tool with these exact arguments:
{args}
""",
        allowed_tools=[f"mcp__plugin_softlight_softlight__{tool}"],
        effort="low",
        fork_session=False,
        json_schema=json_schema,
        model="haiku",
        tools=[],
    )

import argparse
import uuid

from scripts.load_config import load_config
from scripts.post_events import post_events


def setup_project(
    *,
    title: str,
) -> None:
    project_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    print(f"{project_id=}")

    config = load_config(project_id)

    post_events(
        config=config,
        events=[
            {
                "type": "project_updated",
                "title": title,
            },
            {
                "type": "prompt_created",
                "prompt": {
                    "text": """\
Invoke the `run-designer-codegen` skill to create the project and generate the initial explorations.
Do not stop until you have generated every prototype in every exploration that you create in the
project.
""",
                    "key": "generate_prototypes",
                    "effort": "max",
                    "model": "opus",
                },
            },
        ],
    )

    project_url = f"{config.base_url}/projects/{config.project_id}"
    print(f"{project_url=}")


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--title", required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    setup_project(
        title=args.title,

content.content:
import dataclasses
import functools
import json
import pathlib
from typing import Any


@dataclasses.dataclass
class Config:
    """Mutable configuration that is preserved across ``claude`` invocations."""

    base_url: str
    mcp_config: dict[str, Any]
    mcp_config_path: pathlib.Path
    project_id: str
    transcripts: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]


@functools.cache
def load_config(project_id: str) -> Config:
    mcp_config_path = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / ".mcp.json"
    mcp_config = json.loads(mcp_config_path.read_text())
    base_url = mcp_config["mcpServers"]["softlight"]["args"][-1].removesuffix("/mcp/")

    return Config(
        base_url=base_url,
        mcp_config=mcp_config,
        mcp_config_path=mcp_config_path,
        project_id=project_id,
        transcripts={},
    )

content.text:
While the clone runs, let me do the framing work based on my codebase understanding.

**Framing work:**

**Framing A: "There's no at-a-glance org-level view — we need a dashboard."**
- Plausible: every tab is a flat grid. No totals, no charts, no summary. PM said "quick feel."
- Implies: a new Overview tab that aggregates across all orgs with trend + top-N + quota risk.
- Implicates: which orgs get surfaced (top-by-usage vs at-risk), how dense/chart-heavy, how users-within-orgs get shown.

**Framing B: "The Usages table has the right data but flat rows can't roll up or drill down — restructure it."**
- Plausible: Usages already shows org × subscription × metric × usage/limit. We just don't see user attribution. Usage Events has email. They need to be fused.
- Implies: group the Usages grid by org with expandable per-user rows, inline progress bars, and user concentration signal.
- Implicates: could subsume Usage Events tab. Less new surface; reshape what exists.

**Framing C: "The right mental model is 'customer page' — click an org, see everything."**
- Plausible: clicking an account today opens a bare form editor. An admin looking up a customer has to tab-hop and mentally join data. Stripe-dashboard model.
- Implies: rich org detail view with usage trend, top users by activity, subscription/quota/stripe links inline.
- Implicates: flips the index (Accounts tab becomes an entry point rather than the whole story). Usages may stay as portfolio-view.

**Framing D (not committing): "Seat utilization is the real question."**
- This reframes too far from PM's stated problem. Holding.

Committing to A, B, C — they're genuinely different answers to "what should it look like?" A bets on "portfolio glance," B bets on "reshape existing usages table," C bets on "per-customer deep view."

Let me check on the clone status.

input.prompt:
You need to create three explorations on a Softlight canvas via the `mcp__plugin_softlight_softlight__create_exploration` MCP tool. The project_id is `297a25a6-8deb-4223-a9ec-f6cd2a950550`.

Call `mcp__plugin_softlight_softlight__create_exploration` three times with the following arguments. Each call creates an exploration with 3 prototype slots and 3 caption slots.

**Exploration 1:**
- project_id: `297a25a6-8deb-4223-a9ec-f6cd2a950550`
- title: `Portfolio-level overview of org usage`
- description: `A new first-class surface that answers "how much usage is happening across the org portfolio, and within each org, which users are driving it?" — at a glance. Variants trade off how the split manifests visually (ranked cards, treemap concentration, time-series).`
- num_slots: 3
- (and whatever is needed for 3 caption slots — match the tool's schema)

**Exploration 2:**
- project_id: `297a25a6-8deb-4223-a9ec-f6cd2a950550`
- title: `Roll-up and drill-down inside the Usages tab`
- description: `Keep the admin living in grids, but transform the flat Usages table so it rolls up to organization totals and drills down to per-user contribution. Variants trade off how the per-user breakdown surfaces (expandable tree rows, right-side detail panel, inline stacked segments).`
- num_slots: 3

**Exploration 3:**
- project_id: `297a25a6-8deb-4223-a9ec-f6cd2a950550`
- title: `Organization detail page`
- description: `Click an account and see everything about that customer on one page — total usage, quota, trend, and which members are driving activity. Variants trade off what leads (KPI cards, usage chart, or a people-split layout).`
- num_slots: 3

For each call, the MCP tool should return `slot_ids` (list of 3 slot UUIDs) and `caption_slot_ids` (list of 3 caption slot UUIDs).

Return the results in this exact format so I can parse them:

```
EXPLORATION 1 (Portfolio-level overview of org usage)
slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]
caption_slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]

EXPLORATION 2 (Roll-up and drill-down inside the Usages tab)
slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]
caption_slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]

EXPLORATION 3 (Organization detail page)
slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]
caption_slot_ids: [id1, id2, id3]
```

If the tool requires different parameter names, figure out the schema via ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__plugin_softlight_softlight__create_exploration" first. Only call the tool 3 times total. Do not create any other explorations.

28	_EXPLORATION_GAP = 400.0
29	_GRID_SIZE = 40
30	
31	
32	class CreateExplorationOutput(pydantic.BaseModel):
33	    slot_ids: list[str]
34	    """IDs of the created prototype placeholder slots."""
35	
36	    caption_slot_ids: list[str]
37	    """IDs of the created caption placeholder slots."""
38	
39	    title_slot_id: str
40	    """ID of the title slot."""
41	
42	
43	async def _auto_position(
44	    project_id: pydantic.UUID4,
45	    page_id: pydantic.UUID4 | None,
46	) -> tuple[float, float]:
47	    """Compute a position below all existing on-screen slots on the same page."""
48	    project = await routes.get_project(project_id=project_id)
49	    target_page = page_id or None
50	    max_bottom = 0.0
51	    for revision in project.revisions:
52	        for slot in revision.slots:
53	            slot_page = slot.page_id or None
54	            if slot_page != target_page:
55	                continue
56	            if slot.x < -90000 or slot.y < -90000:
57	                continue
58	            bottom = slot.y + (slot.height or 0)
59	            if bottom > max_bottom:
60	                max_bottom = bottom
61	    if max_bottom == 0.0:
62	        return 0.0, 0.0
63	    base_y = math.ceil((max_bottom + _EXPLORATION_GAP) / _GRID_SIZE) * _GRID_SIZE
64	    return 0.0, float(base_y)
65	
66	
67	@tool(output_schema=None)
68	async def create_exploration(
69	    *,
70	    count: Annotated[int, pydantic.Field(ge=1, le=12)],
71	    page_id: pydantic.UUID4 | None = None,
72	    project_id: pydantic.UUID4,
73	    title: str,
74	) -> CreateExplorationOutput:
75	    """Create a titled group of prototype slots with captions below each one."""
76	    slot_ids: list[str] = []
77	    caption_slot_ids: list[str] = []
78	    slot_events: list[Event] = [RevisionCreatedEvent(revision=Revision())]
79	
80	    base_x, current_y = await _auto_position(project_id, page_id)
81	
82	    # Title slot
83	    tid = uuid_mod.uuid4()
84	    title_slot_id = str(tid)
85	    row_width = float(count * _IFRAME_STRIDE - _IFRAME_GAP)
86	    slot_events.append(
87	        SlotCreatedEvent(
88	            slot=SlotModel(
89	                element=TextElement(
90	                    text=title,
91	                    variant="h2",
92	                    bold=True,
93	                ),
94	                metadata=Metadata(id=tid),
95	                page_id=page_id,
96	                width=row_width,
97	                height=float(_TITLE_HEIGHT),
98	                x=base_x,
99	                y=current_y,
100	            ),
101	        ),
102	    )
103	    current_y += _TITLE_HEIGHT + _TITLE_GAP
104	
105	    # Prototype slots
106	    proto_y = current_y
107	    for i in range(count):
108	        slot_id = uuid_mod.uuid4()
109	        slot_ids.append(str(slot_id))
110	        slot_events.append(
111	            SlotCreatedEvent(
112	                slot=SlotModel(
113	                    element=PlaceholderElement(content_type="prototype"),
114	                    metadata=Metadata(id=slot_id),
115	                    page_id=page_id,
116	                    width=float(_IFRAME_WIDTH),
117	                    height=float(_IFRAME_HEIGHT),
118	                    x=base_x + float(i * _IFRAME_STRIDE),
119	                    y=proto_y,
120	                ),
121	            ),
122	        )
123	
124	    # Caption slots
125	    caption_y = proto_y + _IFRAME_HEIGHT + _CAPTION_GAP
126	    for i, proto_slot_id_str in enumerate(slot_ids):
127	        caption_id = uuid_mod.uuid4()
128	        caption_slot_ids.append(str(caption_id))
129	        proto_uuid = uuid_mod.UUID(proto_slot_id_str)
130	        slot_events.append(
131	            SlotCreatedEvent(
132	                slot=SlotModel(
133	                    element=PlaceholderElement(content_type="text"),
134	                    metadata=Metadata(id=caption_id),
135	                    page_id=page_id,
136	                    references=[SlotReference(slot_id=proto_uuid)],
137	                    width=float(_IFRAME_WIDTH),
138	                    height=float(_CAPTION_HEIGHT),
139	                    x=base_x + float(i * _IFRAME_STRIDE),
140	                    y=caption_y,
141	                ),
142	            ),
143	        )
144	
145	    await routes.post_events(project_id=project_id, events=slot_events)
146	
147	    return CreateExplorationOutput(
148	        slot_ids=slot_ids,
149	        caption_slot_ids=caption_slot_ids,
150	        title_slot_id=title_slot_id,
151	    )

content.text: Now dispatching the presenter in the background, then uploading specs and launching prototypes.

input.prompt:
<project_id>297a25a6-8deb-4223-a9ec-f6cd2a950550</project_id>
<thinking>
The PM is working on the billing app and asked for a way to get a quick feel for how much organization usage there's, and how that's split between users. The billing app is an internal admin tool built around 7 AG-Grid tables: Accounts, Subscriptions, Prices, Upgrades, Quotas, Usage Events, Usages. There's no dashboard anywhere. Every tab is a flat grid optimized for CRUD on billing records, not for understanding customers.

When I looked at the data model, the answer to the PM's question was there — but invisible. `accounts` has orgs (name, owner, members array of emails). `usages` has org-level aggregates per (account × subscription × metric × billing period) — so "Wayne Enterprises Pro Plan revisions_generated = 40" is one row. `usage_events` has the raw firehose — every event has an `email` field that tells you which user in the org caused it. So the data to answer "how much does each org use, and who within the org drives it" is all there. The UI just never joins them.

The PM is stuck pivoting between two tables in their head. To see "how much does Acme use?" they go to Usages. To see "who in Acme is doing it?" they have to switch to Usage Events and filter by subscription. And no tab answers "how are things trending." The question feels basic but the tool makes it hard.

I frame this three ways that genuinely disagree about the shape of the solution.

First, there's no at-a-glance surface in the app at all. The PM said "quick feel" — that's a portfolio-glance ask. Every other billing admin tool has a dashboard. We don't. Maybe the whole answer is: build one. Show every org ranked by usage, with per-user breakdowns inline so you can see concentration instantly. This bets on "the tool is missing a top-level surface."

Second, maybe the PM doesn't need a new surface — maybe the Usages tab is already conceptually correct, it just can't roll up to org-level totals or drill down to users. Admins live in these grids. A more conservative, respectful-of-existing-UX answer is to let the Usages grid group by org, expand to show each user's contribution, and render progress bars inline. Less new surface, more transformation of what's there. This bets on "stay native to the pattern that works."

Third, the PM might be asking the wrong unit of question. "How much usage does org X have" is a per-customer question, and the natural UX for that is a customer detail page — open Acme, see everything: usage this period, trend, which of their users are hot, subscription, Stripe link. The current "click an account" behavior opens a dialog for editing metadata; it's not a customer view at all. If you work in admin tools long enough you know the real move is "flip this to a detail page and everything becomes clear." This bets on "reframe the mental model to per-customer, not per-table."

These answer three genuinely different versions of "quick feel." A portfolio at-a-glance. A more capable existing table. A per-customer deep view. Inside each, I'm pushing 3 variants so the PM sees real tension in how the answer could play out — for example, in the portfolio direction, does the primary visual lead with cards (ranking/comparison), a treemap (concentration), or a time-series (growth shape)? Each forces a different conversation.

The variants within each exploration are not "cram every idea into each." They're deliberate bets. Within Portfolio: cards bet on ranking/comparison; treemap bets on visual concentration; time-series bets on trend being the real story. Within Usages-restructure: tree-expand bets on grid-native drill; side panel bets on keeping grid intact plus rich context; inline stacked bar bets on surfacing splits without a click. Within Org detail: KPI-cards-lead bets on the familiar Stripe pattern; chart-leads bets on the chart being the answer; two-column usage-vs-people bets on separating the two PM questions spatially.

The whole set forces the PM to choose not just "which variant" but which *kind of answer* they want — a new dashboard, a richer existing table, or a per-customer page. That's where the real decision lives.
</thinking>
<explorations_created>
Three explorations, each with 3 prototype slots. Every exploration commits to a different framing of "quick feel for org usage and user split."

**Exploration 1: "Portfolio-level overview of org usage"**
Bets on: the billing app needs a new top-level surface that shows every org at a glance with per-user splits baked in.
- slot_ids: [9697b4b9-6972-4f6b-84a3-cbfcf56f9cc9, b6186823-21cc-469d-959d-6bacee657293, 360298f0-44ce-43db-8a3d-389a4d8d0572]
- caption_slot_ids: [18a6b835-0054-4191-bafc-2c44febe785a, 8401c6df-6cf7-4a1e-8e3f-1195094ea393, 6f1b4e15-67d3-4abd-bfc7-1d9f3541dc65]
- Variant A (slot 1, caption 1): Ranked org cards — sortable leaderboard with usage total, quota bar, sparkline, top users inline. Bets on comparison + ranking.
- Variant B (slot 2, caption 2): Treemap of org share — one tile per org sized by usage, colored by quota utilization, drills into per-user sub-tiles. Bets on concentration and risk being visible in one picture.
- Variant C (slot 3, caption 3): Time-first stacked area — usage over time stacked by org with a per-user drill-down chart below. Bets on growth shape being the real story, not cross-sectional snapshots.

**Exploration 2: "Roll-up and drill-down inside the Usages tab"**
Bets on: the Usages tab has the right scope but can't roll up to orgs or drill to users. Fix that rather than adding a new surface.
- slot_ids: [fdf64c72-182e-4538-a7c1-0e9ce9f933d8, 5f9cc5f5-4161-445f-9664-b7f8b2a2e50c, 0f34d3ab-163c-4b2d-a33e-e7e9ddb0ff85]
- caption_slot_ids: [988819bb-389d-4b30-86b0-6ce6bbe44aa4, f52b7a87-5569-46e6-9c7e-d94b1fafac08, 0b39f73e-e076-4de4-a53f-23a59d252af5]
- Variant A (slot 1, caption 1): Grouped tree rows — grid rows group by org, expand to show per-user sub-rows with contribution %. Bets on grid-native drill.
- Variant B (slot 2, caption 2): Right-side detail panel — click a Usages row, side panel slides out with ranked users, per-user trend, quota. Bets on keeping grid intact while adding rich context.
- Variant C (slot 3, caption 3): Inline stacked bar column — each row has a horizontal stacked segment showing per-user split visible at a glance. Bets on surfacing the answer without a click.

**Exploration 3: "Organization detail page"**
Bets on: the real unit of the question is a customer, so build a per-customer page. Click an account, land on a page that answers the whole thing.
- slot_ids: [95c05f82-9a49-4737-a33d-0e2beac7f214, c7c32262-3be0-4f81-a996-888f15f96a02, 59c76b42-3091-4a21-890d-1aac8d52c568]
- caption_slot_ids: [0f91d092-a51a-447a-a60c-9affd4740df0, 1d7961fc-eab6-4874-abef-43e2c8b16019, f67b95b0-cc2e-468c-a2ec-b3c75c7b69d4]
- Variant A (slot 1, caption 1): Stripe-style overview with KPI cards row, trend chart, ranked user table, subscriptions section. Bets on the familiar billing-SaaS pattern.
- Variant B (slot 2, caption 2): Chart leads — giant stacked-by-user usage chart is the hero, everything else contextual. Bets on the chart being the answer.
- Variant C (slot 3, caption 3): Two-column split — left is usage (chart, quota), right is people (ranked members with per-user stats). Bets on spatially separating the two questions the PM is asking.
</explorations_created>

The billing app is an internal admin tool built around 7 AG-Grid tables: Accounts, Subscriptions, Prices, Upgrades, Quotas, Usage Events, Usages. There's no dashboard anywhere. Every tab is a flat grid optimized for CRUD on billing records, not for understanding customers.

When I looked at the data model, the answer to the PM's question was there — but invisible. `accounts` has orgs (name, owner, members array of emails). `usages` has org-level aggregates per (account × subscription × metric × billing period) — so "Wayne Enterprises Pro Plan revisions_generated = 40" is one row. `usage_events` has the raw firehose — every event has an `email` field that tells you which user in the org caused it. So the data to answer "how much does each org use, and who within the org drives it" is all there. The UI just never joins them.

The PM is stuck pivoting between two tables in their head. To see "how much does Acme use?" they go to Usages. To see "who in Acme is doing it?" they have to switch to Usage Events and filter by subscription. And no tab answers "how are things trending." The question feels basic but the tool makes it hard.

I frame this three ways that genuinely disagree about the shape of the solution.

First, there's no at-a-glance surface in the app at all. The PM said "quick feel" — that's a portfolio-glance ask. Every other billing admin tool has a dashboard. We don't. Maybe the whole answer is: build one. Show every org ranked by usage, with per-user breakdowns inline so you can see concentration instantly. This bets on "the tool is missing a top-level surface."

Second, maybe the PM doesn't need a new surface — maybe the Usages tab is already conceptually correct, it just can't roll up to org-level totals or drill down to users. Admins live in these grids. A more conservative, respectful-of-existing-UX answer is to let the Usages grid group by org, expand to show each user's contribution, and render progress bars inline. Less new surface, more transformation of what's there. This bets on "stay native to the pattern that works."

Third, the PM might be asking the wrong unit of question. "How much usage does org X have" is a per-customer question, and the natural UX for that is a customer detail page — open Acme, see everything: usage this period, trend, which of their users are hot, subscription, Stripe link. The current "click an account" behavior opens a dialog for editing metadata; it's not a customer view at all. If you work in admin tools long enough you know the real move is "flip this to a detail page and everything becomes clear." This bets on "reframe the mental model to per-customer, not per-table."

These answer three genuinely different versions of "quick feel." A portfolio at-a-glance. A more capable existing table. A per-customer deep view. Inside each, I'm pushing 3 variants so the PM sees real tension in how the answer could play out — for example, in the portfolio direction, does the primary visual lead with cards (ranking/comparison), a treemap (concentration), or a time-series (growth shape)? Each forces a different conversation.

The variants within each exploration are not "cram every idea into each." They're deliberate bets. Within Portfolio: cards bet on ranking/comparison; treemap bets on visual concentration; time-series bets on trend being the real story. Within Usages-restructure: tree-expand bets on grid-native drill; side panel bets on keeping grid intact plus rich context; inline stacked bar bets on surfacing splits without a click. Within Org detail: KPI-cards-lead bets on the familiar Stripe pattern; chart-leads bets on the chart being the answer; two-column usage-vs-people bets on separating the two PM questions spatially.

The whole set forces the PM to choose not just "which variant" but which *kind of answer* they want — a new dashboard, a richer existing table, or a per-customer page. That's where the real decision lives.
</thinking>

input.command:
cat > /tmp/spec_1_1.json <<'EOF'
{"spec": "Add a new first tab called 'Overview' before 'Accounts', and make it the default tab. This is a portfolio dashboard that shows every organization's usage this billing period with per-user breakdowns inline on each card. The point: a PM glancing at this tab should immediately see 'how much does each org use and who within each org is driving it' without clicking anything.

## Top controls strip (sticky at top of tab)
- Metric segmented control on the left: 'Revisions generated' (default selected) | 'Projects created'
- Period selector dropdown: 'This billing period' (default) | 'Last 30 days' | 'Last billing period'
- Sort dropdown on the right side of the strip: 'Top by usage' (default) | 'Closest to quota' | 'Most active users' | 'Alphabetical'
- Right-aligned small stat strip: three muted stats separated by a vertical divider — 'X orgs active', 'Y revisions total', 'Z active users'.
- The controls row is compact, around 44px tall, with a subtle bottom border.

## Org cards grid
- 3-column grid at 1716px, padded 16px on both sides. Cards auto-wrap.
- Each card is a Radix Card with variant classic. Padding 20px. Border radius default.
- When an org is above 90% of quota, the card gets a red-tinted left border (4px solid var(--red-a7)) and a subtle red glow (box-shadow with red-a3). When 70-90%, amber border.
- Card hover elevates slightly (box-shadow transition).

### Card contents from top to bottom
1. Header row — org name (Radix Text size 4, weight medium), right-aligned priority badge with variant outline (priority 3 = 'High', 1 = 'Medium', 0 = 'Low'), and a small external-link icon button for the Stripe customer dashboard link.
2. Subtitle — 'N members · Pro Plan' in Text size 1 color gray-11. Small grey eye icon if soft-limit has been hit.
3. Hero stat — the big number for this period in Text size 8, weight bold, accent-11 color. Underneath, the metric label 'revisions generated' (Text size 1 gray-11). To the right of the hero, a delta chip: small Badge with ArrowUpIcon or ArrowDownIcon, colored green or red, text '+23% vs prev'.
4. Quota progress bar — horizontal bar, 8px tall, rounded. Filled color shifts green → amber → red past 70% and 90%. Under the bar, a label 'X / Y revisions (Z%)' in Text size 1 gray-11.
5. Sparkline — 60px tall SVG spanning the full width of the card, showing daily counts for this billing period. Use accent color with low opacity fill under the line. A faint horizontal rule at the soft-limit level.
6. Thin divider line.
7. Top contributors section:
   - Section label 'Top contributors' in Text size 1 color gray-11, letter-spacing expanded.
   - Top 3 users as rows. Each row: a small colored circle (deterministic color from the email hash), the email (truncated), a right-aligned count and %, and a horizontal thin progress bar (2px) showing that user's share of the org's total.
   - Footer link '+ N more contributors' when there are more users, styled as a text-only link.

## Mock data (use these 6 orgs matching the baseline clone)
- Wayne Enterprises: priority High, 8 members, Pro Plan. 2,850 / 5,000 revisions (57%). +18%. Top users: bruce@wayne.com (1,240 — 43%), alfred@wayne.com (980 — 34%), lucius@wayne.com (420 — 15%). +2 more.
- Stark Industries: priority High, 12 members, Pro Plan. 4,620 / 5,000 revisions (92%, near limit — show red border and glow). +34%. Top users: tony@stark.com (2,180 — 47%), pepper@stark.com (1,340 — 29%), happy@stark.com (650 — 14%). +3 more.
- Initech Solutions: priority Medium, 9 members, Pro Plan. 3,180 / 5,000 revisions (64%). +5%. Top users: bill@initech.com (1,100 — 35%), peter@initech.com (880 — 28%), samir@initech.com (720 — 23%). +6 more.
- Acme Corp: priority Medium, 5 members, Pro Plan. 1,420 / 5,000 revisions (28%). −12%. Top users: alice@acme.com (820 — 58%), bob@acme.com (380 — 27%), carol@acme.com (220 — 15%).
- Globex Industries: priority Low, 4 members, Free Plan. 890 / 5,000 revisions (18%). +40%. Top users: homer@globex.com (620 — 70%), marge@globex.com (270 — 30%).
- Umbrella Research: priority Low, 3 members, Free Plan. 210 / 5,000 revisions (4%). −50%. Top users: wesker@umbrella.com (180 — 86%), jill@umbrella.com (30 — 14%).

Sort should default to descending by absolute usage, so Stark Industries shows first. When user picks 'Closest to quota', Stark (92%) still wins, but then Initech (64%), Wayne (57%), Acme (28%)...

## Preserve existing tabs
Keep Accounts, Subscriptions, Prices, Upgrades, Quotas, Usage Events, Usages exactly as-is. 'Overview' is a new first tab. Default tab changes from 'accounts' to 'overview'.

## Visual language
Dark theme, use existing Radix Themes tokens from the baseline. Reuse icons from infrastructure/ts/icons (ArrowUpIcon, ArrowDownIcon, ExternalLinkIcon, EllipsisIcon). No Ag-Grid on the Overview tab — hand-built card layout. Match the density of Linear or Vercel dashboards — information-dense but not cramped. Typography should feel native to the existing Radix Themes defaults."}
EOF

input.command:
cat > /tmp/spec_1_2.json <<'EOF'
{"spec": "Add a new first tab called 'Overview' before 'Accounts', and make it the default tab. This Overview tab is a portfolio dashboard where the primary visualization is a single large TREEMAP. One tile per organization; tile area proportional to total usage this billing period; tile color encodes quota utilization. Clicking a tile drills it in place into child sub-tiles for each user within that org.

The bet: treemap makes concentration and risk visible in one picture. A PM can see 'Stark is almost out of quota, Acme is coasting, Wayne has a dominant power user' without sorting or scanning.

## Top controls bar (sticky)
- Metric segmented control: 'Revisions generated' (default) | 'Projects created'
- Period selector dropdown: 'This billing period' (default) | 'Last 30 days' | 'Last billing period'
- Color-encoding legend (right-aligned): a gradient bar labeled '% of quota' with stops at green (0%) → amber (70%) → red (90%) → deep red (100%+). About 200px wide.
- Small right-most text: 'Click an org to see user split'

## Main area — Treemap
- Fills the entire tab content area (minus controls). ~95% width, 70% height of viewport.
- Use d3 squarify or a lightweight hand-rolled treemap algorithm. One rectangle per org, area = usage, gap 2px between tiles.
- Tile interior: org name (truncated with ellipsis), metric count in a subtler secondary line, and a small progress bar or a % badge in the corner.
- Tile color from quota %: 0-40% cool green (var(--grass-a5) to var(--grass-a8)), 40-70% teal/blue, 70-90% amber, 90%+ red.
- Tile border: 1px var(--gray-4); on hover raise border to var(--accent-9) and add a subtle inner glow.
- Bigger tiles get more text detail; tiny tiles just show the name truncated.
- When a tile is clicked, the treemap transitions (400ms): the clicked org's tile stays in place and expands slightly, while its children (users) appear as sub-tiles WITHIN that tile. Sub-tiles have a distinctive look — user initials circle + email + count. Color encoding on sub-tiles switches to 'share of org total' (darker = larger share).
- A 'Back to portfolio' pill appears top-left when drilled in.

## Right-side context panel (only shown on selection)
- When an org tile is selected (clicked once), a 360px-wide right panel slides in showing: org name header, priority badge, subscription info, big usage number, quota progress bar (with soft/hard limits), a 30-day sparkline, and a ranked list of top 5 users with bars. A link to 'Open Stripe customer' at the bottom.
- When no selection, this panel is hidden and the treemap takes the full width.

## Mock data (6 orgs matching the baseline clone)
- Stark Industries: 4,620 revs, 92% of quota (red), 12 members, Pro Plan. Users: tony@stark.com (2,180), pepper@stark.com (1,340), happy@stark.com (650), rhodey@stark.com (320), jarvis@stark.com (130).
- Initech Solutions: 3,180 revs, 64%, 9 members, Pro Plan. Users: bill@initech.com (1,100), peter@initech.com (880), samir@initech.com (720), michael@initech.com (480).
- Wayne Enterprises: 2,850 revs, 57%, 8 members, Pro Plan. Users: bruce@wayne.com (1,240), alfred@wayne.com (980), lucius@wayne.com (420), selina@wayne.com (210).
- Acme Corp: 1,420 revs, 28%, 5 members, Pro Plan. Users: alice@acme.com (820), bob@acme.com (380), carol@acme.com (220).
- Globex Industries: 890 revs, 18% (Free), 4 members. Users: homer@globex.com (620), marge@globex.com (270).
- Umbrella Research: 210 revs, 4% (Free), 3 members. Users: wesker@umbrella.com (180), jill@umbrella.com (30).

Total usage ≈ 13,170 revisions — use this for tile area proportions.

## Preserve existing tabs
All 7 existing tabs stay intact. 'Overview' is the new first tab. Default tab changes from 'accounts' to 'overview'.

## Visual language
Dark theme, match baseline Radix Themes tokens. The treemap should feel modern and clean — think Observable or Finviz-style with smooth transitions. Typography inside tiles must remain legible at small sizes. Use ellipsis aggressively."}
EOF
curl -sF 'file=@/tmp/spec_1_2.json' https://drive.orianna.ai/api/v2/upload

cat > /tmp/spec_1_3.json <<'EOF'
{"spec": "Add a new first tab called 'Overview' before 'Accounts', and make it the default tab. The Overview tab is a TIME-FIRST analytics dashboard. The hero is a large stacked area chart showing usage over time, stacked by organization. Below it sits a compact org table with sparklines. Clicking an org (legend item or table row) changes the lower half of the tab to show a second chart: usage over time for that org, stacked by USER.

The bet: the real story is growth shape, not cross-sectional snapshots. A PM wants to see trend — is Stark accelerating? is Acme slowing down? — with per-user drill-down on demand.

## Top controls (sticky)
- Metric segmented control: 'Revisions generated' (default) | 'Projects created'
- Date range selector (group of pills): 'Last 7 days' | 'Last 30 days' (default) | 'Last billing period' | 'Year to date'
- Stack-by selector: 'Organization' (default) | 'Metric' | 'Plan tier'
- Right-aligned small stat strip: 'X revisions in window', 'Y active orgs', '+N% vs previous window'.

## Main chart — Stacked area over time
- Full-width, ~45% of the tab's vertical space.
- X axis: time. Y axis: daily usage count.
- Stacked areas: top 8 orgs by usage in the window plus 'Other'. Consistent colored strokes using accent-derived hues.
- Interactive cursor vertical line with a rich tooltip showing date + breakdown by org with counts.
- Legend below the chart as a horizontal chip row, with each chip = color swatch + org name + usage count in window. Chips are clickable (single-select); clicked chip highlights that series and triggers the per-user drill-in below.
- A faint horizontal dotted line at the aggregate daily quota ceiling if applicable.
- Subtle chart grid and axis labels. Time axis labels every few days.

## Middle band — Org table with sparklines
- Compact table directly below the main chart.
- Columns: color swatch, org name, plan, usage (window), delta vs prev window (small up/down chip with %), quota %, a wide sparkline column (spanning 200px) showing daily trend, active users count (e.g. '7 of 12').
- Hovering a row highlights the corresponding series in the main chart. Clicking a row (or a legend chip) selects that org.
- 6 rows shown (all orgs).
- Table is dense, similar visual weight to AG-Grid rows but custom-styled. Sticky header.

## Lower section — Per-user drill (appears only when an org is selected)
- When an org is selected, a section expands beneath the table (with a smooth transition) titled 'Breakdown within <OrgName>'.
- Inside this section: a second stacked area chart, same time range, but stacked by USER email (top 5 users + 'Other').
- To the right of this second chart (if width allows), a ranked user list with email, total count in window, and a thin horizontal bar showing share.
- A 'Collapse' link in the top-right of this section that dismisses the drill back to the portfolio view.
- If no org is selected, this section shows a subtle empty state: 'Select an organization to see per-user breakdown.'

## Mock data (use these 6 orgs matching the baseline clone; fabricate daily counts with realistic shapes)
- Stark Industries (accelerating): gradual ramp, ~150/day average, ~4,620 total (92% quota), 12 members. Top users this period: tony@stark.com (2,180), pepper@stark.com (1,340), happy@stark.com (650), rhodey@stark.com (320), jarvis@stark.com (130).
- Initech Solutions (steady): flat-ish ~100/day, ~3,180 total (64%). Users: bill@initech.com, peter@initech.com, samir@initech.com, michael@initech.com.
- Wayne Enterprises (steady-slight-growth): ~95/day, ~2,850 total (57%).
- Acme Corp (declining): dropped off in 2nd half, ~1,420 total (28%).
- Globex Industries (spiky growth from low base): ~890 total (18% — Free plan).
- Umbrella Research (declining): ~210 total (4% — Free plan).

## Preserve existing tabs
All 7 existing tabs stay intact. 'Overview' is the new first tab. Default tab changes from 'accounts' to 'overview'.

## Visual language
Dark theme, match baseline Radix Themes tokens. Use a lightweight chart library (recharts or visx) — install as needed. Charts should feel like Datadog or Grafana — clean, dense, legible, with subtle grid lines and strong accent color use for the selected series."}
EOF
curl -sF 'file=@/tmp/spec_1_3.json' https://drive.orianna.ai/api/v2/upload

cat > /tmp/spec_2_1.json <<'EOF'
{"spec": "Transform the existing 'Usages' tab. The current Usages tab is a flat AG-Grid where each row is one (account × subscription × metric × billing period). Restructure it into a hierarchical grid where rows group by ORGANIZATION, with expandable CHILD ROWS for each USER inside the org, using AG-Grid tree data or master-detail.

The bet: admins already live in these grids. Don't build a new surface — teach this grid to roll up to orgs and drill down to users natively.

## Top of tab
- Keep the existing AG-Grid look, but add a small header strip above the grid:
  - Metric filter pills: 'All metrics' (default) | 'Revisions generated' | 'Projects created'
  - Billing period selector: 'Current' (default) | dropdown of recent periods
  - A small legend on the right: 'Expand a row to see per-user breakdown'
- The grid below takes the remaining space.

## Grid structure (three levels)

### Level 1 — Org rows (default view)
Columns:
- Expand caret (chevron right; rotates down when expanded)
- Organization: org name with a colored initials avatar to the left
- Plan: badge showing the plan tier
- Metric: which metric this row aggregates for
- Usage: big number, aggregated across all users for this org/metric/period
- Soft Limit: number
- Hard Limit: number
- Usage bar: a horizontal progress bar in the cell (custom cellRenderer) colored green/amber/red by quota %. Right-aligned % text.
- Active users: count like '7 of 12'
- Period: 'Mar 1 - Mar 31'

### Level 2 — User child rows (revealed when a Level 1 row is expanded)
Rendered as indented sub-rows with a slightly different background (var(--gray-2)). No avatar on user rows — instead a small colored dot derived from the email hash.
Columns align with the parent but:
- Expand caret hidden
- 'Organization' column shows the user email (indented, smaller)
- 'Plan' column is blank
- 'Metric' column is blank (inherited)
- 'Usage' shows this user's count
- Soft/Hard Limit blank
- 'Usage bar' shows the user's share of the ORG'S usage (thinner, different color — purple-based), with '% of org' label
- 'Active users' blank
- 'Period' blank
Users are sorted by contribution descending. Top 5 shown by default; below them a 'Show N more users' link that expands the rest.

## Behavior
- Clicking the expand caret toggles children with a smooth animation (AG Grid's row group or master-detail).
- Clicking an expanded user row opens a small dialog/drawer with the user's events in this period.
- Sorting the grid at level 1 sorts orgs. Users under each org stay sorted by contribution.
- Filtering (floating filter row) still works at level 1.
- Support searching by email globally — if any email matches, auto-expand the parent org.

## Mock data (use baseline clone's 6 orgs; show aggregate org-row with children)
- Stark Industries · Pro Plan · revisions_generated: 4,620 / 5,000 (92%, RED bar, near limit). Children: tony@stark.com 2,180 (47%), pepper@stark.com 1,340 (29%), happy@stark.com 650 (14%), rhodey@stark.com 320 (7%), jarvis@stark.com 130 (3%).
- Initech Solutions · Pro Plan · revisions_generated: 3,180 / 5,000 (64%). Children: bill@initech.com 1,100 (35%), peter@initech.com 880 (28%), samir@initech.com 720 (23%), michael@initech.com 480 (15%).
- Wayne Enterprises · Pro Plan · revisions_generated: 2,850 / 5,000 (57%). Children: bruce@wayne.com 1,240 (43%), alfred@wayne.com 980 (34%), lucius@wayne.com 420 (15%), selina@wayne.com 210 (7%).
- Acme Corp · Pro Plan · revisions_generated: 1,420 / 5,000 (28%). Children: alice@acme.com 820 (58%), bob@acme.com 380 (27%), carol@acme.com 220 (15%).
- Globex Industries · Free · revisions_generated: 890 / 5,000 (18%). Children: homer@globex.com 620 (70%), marge@globex.com 270 (30%).
- Umbrella Research · Free · revisions_generated: 210 / 5,000 (4%). Children: wesker@umbrella.com 180 (86%), jill@umbrella.com 30 (14%).
Also include a single 'projects_created' row per org with slightly lower counts to show the metric dimension.

## Preserve other tabs
All other tabs (Accounts, Subscriptions, Prices, Upgrades, Quotas, Usage Events) stay unchanged. Only the Usages tab is redesigned.

## Visual language
Keep the dark AG-Grid theme intact. Custom cellRenderers for: the initials avatar, the progress bar column, and the user's share bar. Rows slightly taller than default (~40px) to give breathing room for the bars. Zebra striping stays off; row hover stays. The existing 'Refresh Usage' button at the footer stays too."}
EOF

{"spec": "Transform the existing 'Usages' tab. Keep the flat AG-Grid intact — same columns, same sort/filter/refresh behavior. But when a user clicks any row, a rich DETAIL PANEL slides in from the right side of the screen over the grid (like a Radix Dialog with side placement). That panel is where the per-user breakdown, trend chart, and quota context live.

The bet: admins love the grid and want to keep it. Don't restructure the data — add a layer on top that answers 'who's driving this org' on demand.

## Grid (mostly unchanged)
- Keep all existing columns: Account, Subscription, Price, Quota, Metric, Soft Limit, Hard Limit, Usage, Usage (%), Starts At, Ends At.
- Add a small 'Progress' column (custom cellRenderer) between 'Usage' and 'Usage (%)': a horizontal colored progress bar (green/amber/red based on % of hard limit).
- Row hover highlights. Click-to-open behavior is new: clicking any row opens the right-side detail panel.
- Add a subtle 'chevron right' icon at the far right of each row to hint at the drill-in affordance.

## Right-side detail panel (the main change)
When a row is selected, a 480px-wide panel slides in from the right. Grid content shrinks to the left, not overlaid. Panel has a close button top-right and persists until dismissed. Inside, from top to bottom:

### Header block
- Back arrow + org name (h5), subtitle 'Pro Plan · revisions_generated · billing period Mar 1 – Mar 31'
- Right-aligned: a compact 'Open in Stripe' button

### Big stats row (two columns)
- Left stat: 'Usage' — big number 4,620. Small label 'of 5,000'.
- Right stat: 'Active users' — big number 5. Small label 'of 12 members'.

### Quota bar
- Horizontal bar, 12px tall, rounded. Tick marks at soft limit (e.g. 3,500) and hard limit (5,000). Filled region colored by % (green/amber/red).
- Below it, two labels: left 'Soft limit 3,500 (exceeded)' with a warning pill, right '92% of hard limit'.

### 30-day trend chart
- Small multiline area chart showing daily usage for the org over the period, plus a dashed line for the soft limit.
- 160px tall, full width of the panel minus padding.

### Divider with label 'Breakdown by user (5 active)'

### Ranked user list
- Table-ish list. Each row:
  - Colored circle with initials (hash-based color)
  - Email (truncated if long)
  - Usage count right-aligned
  - Below the email, a horizontal thin bar showing user's share of the org total, with the % at the end
  - A tiny '→' on the rightmost edge to open 'View events for this user' (stubbed)
- Show all active users (5 for Stark, 4 for Initech, etc.) sorted descending by usage.
- Inactive members (who have 0 events this period) appear below a subtle divider with muted styling — 'No activity this period'.

## Mock data (same 6 orgs as baseline clone)
When Stark Industries row is clicked (default demo state), show:
- Usage 4,620 / 5,000 (92% — red bar)
- Active users 5 of 12
- Trend line: gentle upslope over 30 days
- Users: tony@stark.com 2,180 (47%), pepper@stark.com 1,340 (29%), happy@stark.com 650 (14%), rhodey@stark.com 320 (7%), jarvis@stark.com 130 (3%)
- Inactive members: rogers@stark.com, romanoff@stark.com, barton@stark.com, etc. (7 users with 0 events)
The prototype should render with Stark Industries pre-selected so the panel is visible on first render. But also support clicking other rows to swap the panel content.

## Preserve other tabs
All other tabs stay unchanged.

## Visual language
Dark theme, Radix Themes. Panel should feel like a polished side drawer (Linear issue panel vibes). Use existing icons: ChevronRight, ExternalLink, X. Small shadow + backdrop on the panel. Grid still feels primary; panel is rich but clearly secondary. Use recharts or a simple SVG for the 30-day trend."}
EOF
curl -sF 'file=@/tmp/spec_2_2.json' https://drive.orianna.ai/api/v2/upload

cat > /tmp/spec_2_3.json <<'EOF'
{"spec": "Transform the existing 'Usages' tab. Keep the flat AG-Grid structure but add a dominant new column ('User split') that renders an INLINE HORIZONTAL STACKED BAR showing each row's per-user breakdown directly in the cell. No click needed — the answer to 'who within this org is driving usage' is visible at a glance across every row.

The bet: the most powerful expression of 'how usage is split across users' is to make the split itself the primary visual in each row. No drill-down, no panel — just show it.

## Top of tab
- Small header strip above the grid:
  - Metric filter pills: 'All metrics' (default) | 'Revisions generated' | 'Projects created'
  - A legend explaining the inline bar: a mini preview showing 3 colored segments labeled 'top contributors' with a '+' label for 'others'.

## Grid column changes
Reorder and update columns to put the new bar front-and-center:
1. Account — org name
2. Plan — badge
3. Metric
4. Usage — big number
5. Progress — wide column (~240px) with a colored horizontal progress bar (green/amber/red) showing % of hard limit, with label overlay '4,620 / 5,000 (92%)'
6. **User split** — NEW, the widest column (~400px). Horizontal stacked bar showing per-user contribution, each segment colored distinctly, segments sorted by size largest-first. Hover a segment to show tooltip with email + count + %.
7. Active users — small badge like '5 of 12'
8. Period
- Remove redundant columns like 'Subscription ID', 'Price ID', 'Quota ID' from default view — they clutter the core answer.

## User split column detail
- Height of the stacked bar: 16px. Rounded corners. Thin white/gray hairlines between segments.
- Segments are colored by deterministic hash of email. Top 4 contributors get saturated accent-derived colors; anything beyond top 4 is collapsed into a single grey 'Others' segment.
- On hover, a tooltip follows the mouse, showing: user email (bold), count, and % of org.
- Below the bar, a tiny legend line in 10px type showing the top 3 user emails truncated (e.g. 'tony@stark.com 47% · pepper@… 29% · happy@… 14% · +2 more').
- Row height increases to ~56px to accommodate the bar + legend.

## Scanning behavior
With this layout, a PM can scan down the grid and see:
- Stark's bar is mostly one dominant segment (tony) — high concentration
- Initech's bar has 4 roughly-equal segments — even distribution
- Globex's bar is 70/30 — very concentrated
Concentration is readable at a glance without clicking anything.

## Mock data (same 6 orgs as baseline clone)
- Stark Industries · Pro · revisions_generated: 4,620/5,000 (92% red). Split: tony@stark.com 2,180 (47%), pepper@stark.com 1,340 (29%), happy@stark.com 650 (14%), rhodey@stark.com 320 (7%), jarvis@stark.com 130 (3%).
- Initech Solutions · Pro · revisions_generated: 3,180/5,000 (64%). Split: bill@initech.com 1,100 (35%), peter@initech.com 880 (28%), samir@initech.com 720 (23%), michael@initech.com 480 (15%).
- Wayne Enterprises · Pro · revisions_generated: 2,850/5,000 (57%). Split: bruce@wayne.com 1,240 (43%), alfred@wayne.com 980 (34%), lucius@wayne.com 420 (15%), selina@wayne.com 210 (7%).
- Acme Corp · Pro · revisions_generated: 1,420/5,000 (28%). Split: alice@acme.com 820 (58%), bob@acme.com 380 (27%), carol@acme.com 220 (15%).
- Globex Industries · Free · revisions_generated: 890/5,000 (18%). Split: homer@globex.com 620 (70%), marge@globex.com 270 (30%).
- Umbrella Research · Free · revisions_generated: 210/5,000 (4%). Split: wesker@umbrella.com 180 (86%), jill@umbrella.com 30 (14%).
Include one additional row per org for 'projects_created' metric, with smaller numbers and the same user split color-coding.

## Interactions
- Clicking a row still works — opens a simple read-only modal (inheriting existing UsageEventEditor pattern) with more details. But the primary interaction is scanning.
- Sort-by: default 'Usage desc'. Add a sort option 'Most concentrated' that sorts by the largest segment's share (Gini-ish).

## Preserve other tabs
All other tabs stay unchanged.

## Visual language
Dark theme, AG-Grid. Custom cellRenderer for the user-split column. Segment colors should be visually distinct but harmonious — use a palette derived from Radix accent plus purple/teal/crimson for variety. Hover tooltips use Radix Tooltip. Match the rest of the grid's density and typography."}
EOF
curl -sF 'file=@/tmp/spec_2_3.json' https://drive.orianna.ai/api/v2/upload
